meta_title: For Students: testing automation to Solve no version control (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to testing automation and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Testing Automation to Solve No Version Control
content.seo_description: Learn how to use testing automation to overcome the lack of version control. A beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to secure your code.
content.answer_block: Without version control, your code is one accidental delete away from disaster. Testing automation acts as a safety net, verifying your logic before you break it. By integrating Antigravity and Claude Code, you create a robust, self-validating development loop that ensures every change is stable, even without Git.
content.intro:
You are staring at a folder full of files named 'final', 'final_v2', and 'final_real_final'. The anxiety is mounting because you know one wrong keystroke could wipe out hours of progress, and you have no way to revert to a working state. You are building in the dark, hoping the code holds together.

But what if your code could tell you the moment it breaks? By treating testing as your primary safety mechanism, you shift from 'hope-based' development to 'proof-based' engineering. This guide will teach you how to automate your validation flow, giving you the confidence to refactor and expand your projects without the fear of losing everything.

steps.title: Define Your Critical Path
steps.description: Identify the one function that, if it fails, ruins the entire app. This is your first target for automated testing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase and identify the most critical functional path. Generate a list of edge cases that could cause this path to fail.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Identify critical functions and list edge cases'

steps.title: Build the Test Harness
steps.description: Create a lightweight test runner that executes your core logic against predefined inputs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Python test harness that imports my main logic and runs it against the edge cases identified in step 1.
steps.claude_code_command: claude build --file test_harness.py --logic 'run critical path tests'

steps.title: Automate Execution Hooks
steps.description: Trigger your test suite automatically before you modify any core files.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a wrapper script that runs the test harness before executing the main application logic.

steps.title: Implement State Snapshotting
steps.description: Since you lack version control, create a script to copy your current working directory to a 'stable' folder whenever tests pass.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a script that clones the current directory to a 'backup_stable' folder only if the test harness returns a success status.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create conditional backup script for stable builds'

steps.title: Continuous Validation Loop
steps.description: Use Claude Code to monitor your files and re-run tests every time a file is saved.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a background process that watches for file system changes and triggers the test harness immediately.

pro_tips: Always write your tests before you write the new feature code to ensure you understand the requirements.
Use mock data for your tests so you don't rely on external APIs that might change or fail.
Name your backup folders with timestamps so you have a chronological trail even without Git.
Keep your test suite under 5 seconds of execution time to maintain your flow state.
Delete your oldest stable backup folder once you have five verified working versions to save disk space.
content.next_steps: Once your testing loop is stable, start documenting your API endpoints in a README file to prepare for professional collaboration.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation to protect your code and scale your development velocity like a pro. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
